summary: Design a family of SOTA CNN architectures (ConvNeXt/ResNeXt-style) for ESC-50 audio spectrogram classification, parameterized across 5M, 10M, 20M, and 30M sizes. The design uses mel-spectrogram inputs, depthwise separable convolutions, squeeze-and-excitation blocks, and proper regularization (SpecAugment, Mixup, Label Smoothing) to achieve strong generalization on the small ESC-50 dataset.
assumptions: Input is 64-channel mel-spectrograms (hop length 512, 5-second clips at 22kHz → ~215 time frames, resized/padded to 224×224 for compatibility with image-CNN backbones)
The model family scales depth and width (stages and channel multipliers) while keeping stem/head fixed; no attention/transformer components per the 'CNN' constraint
ESC-50's small size (2,000 samples) requires aggressive augmentation and transfer learning from AudioSet-pretrained weights to avoid overfitting
